A.missing: no agent disrupting; established order rendered only as generic long-happening thing, no 'order' or 'fails to continue' via agentive disruption
A.reason: HAPPEN-based skeleton: a long-standing thing can no longer happen; lacks the agentive disrupting of an established order.

B.missing: no BECAUSE tying event to failure, no agent; just something long-happening then cannot happen
B.reason: Bare 'long thing can no longer happen'; missing agentive disruption and established-order sense.

C.missing: no external disrupter; PEOPLE stop doing what they long did, reads as spontaneous cessation not act of disrupting
C.reason: Renders many-people-stop-doing but the 'act of disrupting' by something/someone is absent; genus of disruption not carried.

D.missing: someone DOes to the thing, but no BECAUSE linking that act to it no longer being able to happen; causal disruption unlinked
D.reason: Adds an agent acting on the long-standing thing plus its ceasing, closer to disrupting an order, but causal chain from act to failure is loose.

E.missing: someone acts and thing ceases but no BECAUSE joining the act to the ceasing; disruption causation not asserted
E.reason: Agent acts on established thing that then cannot happen; near-faithful but the causal 'so it fails' link is only juxtaposed.

F.missing: no agent disrupting and no causal link; only PEOPLE long did X then cannot do X
F.reason: People long did a thing then cannot; lacks the disrupting act and BECAUSE causation of established-order failure.
